author: siladu
content:
<@259648573401071617> Besu team generally supports rolling window with minimal block retention, enough to keep protocol safe. 4-5 months minimum was also suggested.

Agree needs to be at least >= WSP.
Personally think we should also retain all unfinalized blocks if we have a long non-finalized period, which in theory could be greater than whatever hardcoded value we might pick.

Besu has an experimental flag `--Xchain-pruning-enabled` with a configurable number of blocks `--Xchain-pruning-blocks-retained` which has an (overridable) minimum of `3533 epochs * 32 slots per epoch = 113056` <-- this is BAL retention period AFAIK, which is less than current WSP.

We would be happy to store more blocks than WSP though if there's a good reason to. Seems useful to match CL retention policy.
